_provenance: Transcribed from the ACTUAL Colab run of scripts/02_interface_metrics.py on the Step-1 Boltz outputs (Boltz-2 ran on a Colab T4; raw .cif/affinity outputs live on Colab and are gitignored as heavy artifacts). These are the real values that run produced (Cell-8 metric panel). To regenerate the canonical artifact, re-run scripts/01_boltz_smoketest.py + scripts/02_interface_metrics.py on Colab and it will write runs/step1_boltz/specificity-style outputs + a runlog. This file exists so the lysozyme/ipTM/pDockQ result cited elsewhere is backed by a committed artifact, not only chat history.
_what_it_shows: Raw ipTM and pDockQ are FOOLED by confident non-binders: the scrambled decoy out-scored the real TRAIL binder on ipTM (0.859 vs 0.769), and the folded non-binder lysozyme TIED the real binder on pDockQ (0.742 vs 0.742). Only the two-axis filter (interface pLDDT high AND inter-chain PAE low) separates the real binder. This is the firsthand evidence behind the two-axis interface filter used project-wide.

two_axis_verdict.conclusion: Only the real binder passes both axes — raw ipTM/pDockQ alone do not discriminate.
